description: List of crime incidents featured in the Cambridge Police Department’s Annual Crime Reports and reported in the City of Cambridge since 2009. Includes more than 40 crime types. Certain crime types are excluded due to confidentiality and/or protection of privacy. Please Note: Addresses do not represent the actual location of the crime, but a near approximation within 100 block ranges. For reports published by the Cambridge Police Department’s Crime Analysis Unit, visit http://www.cambridgema.gov/c

name: Police Department Crash Data - Updated

columns.name: May Involve Pedestrian
columns.description: This calculated field indicates whether each crash might involve cyclist. The field is calculated by searching for the text string "pedestrian" within each column for a given row. The keyword "walk" was not used so as to avoid the risk of false positives from the phrase "walk signal." This new column has been included to expedite analysis of the City's crash data for bicycle safety. We are confident that it has caught the vast majority of pedestrian related incidents in this dataset, but please be advised that no text search algorithm is perfect, and this one may not account for 100 percent of pedestrian-related incidents.

columns.name: P2 Non Motorist Desc
columns.description: When Role of involved person is NON-MOTORIST (CYCLIST, PEDESTRIAN, etc.)

columns.name: V1 Model
columns.description: The manufacturer assigned name denoting a family of Vehicles (within a make) which has a degree of similarity in construction, such as body, chassis, etc. (e.g., “CIVIC,” “TAURUS”)

columns.name: Trafficway Description
columns.description: Indicates whether or not a trafficway is divided and whether it serves one-way or two-way traffic. (e.g., “One-way, not divided,” “Two-way, divided”)

columns.name: Road Surface Condition
columns.description: The apparent condition of the road. (e.g., “Wet,” “Dry,” “Snow”)

columns.name: Traffic Control Device Type
columns.description: Indicates whether the traffic control was functioning at the time of Crash

columns.name: Weather Condition 2
columns.description: This data attribute is captured only if there is more than one weather condition type needed to be captured. (e.g., Weather Condition 1 = “Cloudy”; Weather Condition 2 = “Rain”)

columns.name: First Harmful Event Location
columns.description: The injury or damage producing event which characterizes the Crash type and identifies the nature of the first harmful event. (e.g., “Collision with motor vehicle,” “Collision with guardrail”)

columns.name: Object 2
columns.description: Second involved object in this accident (type of vehicle).

columns.name: Object 1
columns.description: First involved object in this accident (type of vehicle).

columns.name: Date Time
columns.description: Date and Time accident occurred (12:00 AM may represent an unknown time)

columns.name: P1 Non Motorist Desc
columns.description: When Role of involved person is NON-MOTORIST (CYCLIST, PEDESTRIAN, etc.)

columns.name: Weather Condition 1
columns.description: The weather condition (e.g., “Cloudy,” “Rain,” “Snow”) at the time of the Crash.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Crash location: either an intersection, address, or nearby street. Locations have been geocoded when possible.

columns.name: May involve cyclist
columns.description: This calculated field indicates whether each crash might involve cyclist. The field is calculated by searching for the text strings "bicycle," "pedalcycle," or "cycli" within each column for a given row. The keyword "cycle" was not used so as to avoid the risk of false positives from the word "motorcycle." This new column has been included to expedite analysis of the City's crash data for bicycle safety. We are confident that it has caught the vast majority of bicycle related incidents in this dataset, but please be advised that no text search algorithm is perfect, and this one may not account for 100 percent of bicycle-related incidents.

columns.name: Roadway Junction Type
columns.description: A code which uniquely identifies a roadway junction type. A junction is either an intersection or the connection between a driveway access and a roadway other than a driveway access. (e.g., “T-intersection,” “Four-way intersection”)

columns.name: School Bus Related
columns.description: Indicates whether a school bus is related to the Crash. The school bus must be directly involved as a contact vehicle or indirectly involved as a non-contact vehicle

columns.name: V1 Model Year
columns.description: The year which is assigned to a Vehicle by the manufacturer

columns.name: V1 Make
columns.description: The distinctive name applied to the Vehicle by the manufacturer. (e.g., “HONDA,” “FORD”)

columns.name: V2 Model Year
columns.description: The year which is assigned to a Vehicle by the manufacturer

columns.name: V2 Model
columns.description: The manufacturer assigned name denoting a family of Vehicles (within a make) which has a degree of similarity in construction, such as body, chassis, etc. (e.g., “CIVIC,” “TAURUS”)

columns.name: V2 Make
columns.description: The distinctive name applied to the Vehicle by the manufacturer. (e.g., “HONDA,” “FORD”)

description: List of crashes involving motor vehicles, bicycles and/or pedestrians reported in the City of Cambridge since 2015. Any data or text that may identify specific persons or companies has been omitted. Please see attached MassTRAC documentation for additional explanation about crash reporting data elements. Note: crashes involving pedestrians are indicated in the “P1 Non Motorist Desc” and “P2 Mon Motorist Desc” columns. When using Socrata's built in visualization tools to map crashes, please use t

name: Eco-Totem Broadway Bicycle Count

columns.name: DateTime
columns.description: The Broadway Eco Totem tracks the number of bicycles that pass by during fifteen minute increments. Numbers in this column signify the start of each increment. This field is used as a unique ID for the data.

columns.name: Date
columns.description: The date (MM-DD-YYYY) that the counts were made.

columns.name: Time
columns.description: The Ecototem counts the number of bicyclists that pass by in 15 minute increments. This column indicates the starting time (HHMMSS) of each 15 minute increment.

columns.name: Day
columns.description: The day of the week that the counts were made.

columns.name: Total
columns.description: The total number of bicyclists riding through the Kendall Square area on Broadway, both eastbound and westbound.

columns.name: Eastbound
columns.description: The total number of bicyclists riding eastbound through Kendall Square on Broadway.

columns.name: Westbound
columns.description: The total number of bicyclists riding westbound through Kendall Square on Broadway.

description: NOTE: Due to construction on Broadway, we have paused data collection on the Eco-Totem as of June 30, 2024. When construction is complete we will resume updating this dataset. The “Eco-Totem” counter (made by the Montreal-based company Eco-Counter) counts cyclists via in-ground loop detectors embedded under the pavement of Broadway near Kendall Square. The device displays on a monitor how many cyclists pass by, and data is transferred in 15 minute increments daily from the device to Cambridge's

name: Cambridge Parking Tickets
provider: This data resides on the City of Cambridge Ticket Managment System currently managed by Xerox, Corp.

columns.name: Department
columns.description: The municipal department that issued the ticket.

columns.name: Violation Description
columns.description: The Violation Description is the officer's or issuer's best description of the actual violation or infraction and often makes reference to the posted sign or display that indicates what is wrong. No Stopping or No Parking refer to the posted sign visible to the driver that the area is restricted in some way. Expired Meter indicates the meter is flashing or displays an expired indicator. Overtime would indicate the vehicle has exceeded the allotted time allowed to park

columns.name: Meter
columns.description: This column contains data when the violation is for an Expired Meter. It lists the actual meter number that is visible on the meter's post or visible on a label that appears within the meter's glass-globe.

columns.name: Issue Time
columns.description: This column contains the actual time the violation was issued. The format is HH:MM and an indicator of AM or PM

columns.name: Ticket Issue Date
columns.description: This is the actual calendar date when the violation was issued. Even Legal Holidays will appear on some violation data since Police will patrol the streets even on these days.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: This column contains the officer's or issuer's best description of where the violation occurred. Violations often need to be described only as a Street Number and Street Name. Others need to describe an intersection of two streets or that the violation was observed Opposite to Street Number and Name. Some may even describe a generic location such as a Park or Mall.  An Asterisk will indicate the location is a Parking Meter at Street Number and Name. Because of changes in the open data platform's geocoding application, some locations were replaced with blanks in this field of the open dataset if the location could not be geocoded.

columns.name: amount due
columns.description: amount of money owed on ticket. this field was added in 2025.

description: This layer contains the Parking Tickets issued since January 2014 and contains tickets that were issued by both the PCO's, the Parking Control Officers, and the Cambridge Police. It does not contain Ticket Numbers or Officer Badge Numbers at the instruction of management. Nor does it contain any vehicle data or plate numbers which could inadvertently compromise private data.

description: This dataset includes Point-in-Time (PIT) data collected in Cambridge between 2012 and 2025. The PIT count is a count of sheltered and unsheltered homeless persons on a single night in January. The U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) requires that communities receiving funding through the Continuum of Care (CoC) Program conduct an annual count of homeless persons on a single night in the last 10 days of January, and these data contribute to national estimates of homelessness r

columns.name: Data Year
columns.description: The year in which the energy and water in the report was used. Properties subject to BEUDO must report their data for the prior year (the Data Year) by May of the following year.

columns.name: Buildings Included
columns.description: The list of building IDs included in this report. Building IDs come from the Cambridge GIS building footprints layer. The IDs included in a given data year represent the building IDs as they existed at that time. Building IDs may have changed over time, even though they refer to the same building. You can ensure comparability across years by comparing properties with the same Reporting ID, even if the list of buildings included may change for different data years.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: The longitude of the point assigned for the location of this report. If a report represents a single building, this is the center of the building footprint. If a report represents multiple buildings, this is the center of the parcel.

columns.name: Coordinates
columns.description: Combined latitude/longitude point for use in Open Data mapping

columns.name: Reporting ID
columns.description: The ID assigned to this report. Building IDs (Buildings Included) and parcel IDs (MapLot) can change over time even when there are no changes to the actual buildings. If there are significant changes for a subject property, such as buildings being demolished, new buildings constructed, or changes in parcel boundaries that change the buildings included on the parcel, a property will be assigned a new Reporting ID. When comparing data across multiple data years, you should only make direct comparisons between the same reporting IDs.

columns.name: MapLot
columns.description: The map-lot ID locator for the property. Some reports include buildings on multiple map/lots.

columns.name: BEUDO Category
columns.description: Properties may be subject to the BEUDO ordinance under three categories: Residential (50+ units), Non-Residential (25,000+ SF), or Municipal (municipal properties 10,000+ SF).

columns.name: PD Parcel Living Area
columns.description: The total living area of all buildings on the parcel from the Property Database. The report itself may not include all buildings on the parcel or may include buildings on other parcels as well.

columns.name: PD Parcel Units
columns.description: The total number of residential units in all buildings on the parcel (MapLot) from the Property Database. The report itself may not include all buildings on the parcel or may include buildings on other parcels as well.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: The street address assigned to this report. Reports may cover multiple buildings with multiple addresses.

columns.name: Year Built
columns.description: The value entered in ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager for the construction year. Reports may include multiple buildings with different construction years.

columns.name: Buildings Included Count
columns.description: The count of building IDs in Buildings Included field.

columns.name: Primary Property Type - Self Selected

description: Use Cambridge's open data to help our city come up with innovative solutions to its biggest challenges. This dataset lists city issues that you can help us solve by analyzing or hacking on our open data. It's certainly not an exhaustive list, but we hope it will at least point you in the right direction. Feel free to reach out at OpenData@cambridgema.gov with questions or ideas. Thanks for your help. We're glad you're on our team!

columns.description: A brief narrative summary of the incident as reported by the responding officer or staff.

columns.description: The street or intersection where the incident occurred or was reported.

columns.description: A more specific classification of the report, often referencing Massachusetts General Laws (MGL). For example, “SHOPLIFTING BY ASPORTATION C266 S30A” refers to Chapter 266, Section 30A of the MGL.

columns.description: A unique identifier assigned to each police report or incident log entry.

columns.name: Date & Time
columns.description: The date and time the police response or report was initiated.

description: The Daily Police Log offers a comprehensive overview of incidents reported to and by the Cambridge Police Department (CPD). Each entry represents a distinct call for service or police report, encompassing a wide range of public safety activities, from criminal offenses to non-criminal matters. To protect the privacy of individuals involved, personal identifiers are omitted from the dataset, and location information is limited to the street or block level rather than precise addresses. columns.name: Note

columns.name: Year Complete
columns.description: Refers to the year in which a Certificate of Occupancy is granted.

columns.name: Parking Spaces
columns.description: Includes only on site spaces assigned to uses found in the project. In some case parking is shared with other developments or is located off site. In some instances parking for other nearby uses is located on site. In these cases information about total parking found on the project parcel is stated in the Notes field.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: Primary street address of project. The street address of a project may change once a land subdivision takes place or an official address is assigned by the City of Cambridge.

columns.name: Project Name
columns.description: Refers to a name generally used to refer to the project.  Not all projects have names.

columns.name: Geocode Address
columns.description: Address used to geocode project for placement on map.  In some cases differs from the street address to accommodate mapping process.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: Latitude of project location

columns.name: Project Stage
columns.description: Refers to the project stage.  Options are Permitting, Required Design Review Underway, Zoning Permit Granted or As of Right, Building Permit Granted, and Complete. See metadata for further details.

columns.name: Project Description
columns.description: A short description of the project.

columns.name: Coordinates
columns.description: Latitude and longitude used to map project location.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: Longitude of project location

columns.name: Project Type
columns.description: Refers to the type of construction called for by the project. Options include addition, alteration, change of use, master plan, and new construction.

columns.name: Permit Type
columns.description: Refers to the primary type of development approval required from the City of Cambridge. Options include Planning Board Special Permit, As of Right, Board of Zoning Appeal, Comprehensive Permit, and large Project Review.

columns.name: Zoning
columns.description: Primary zoning category under which the project receives(d) permits.

columns.name: Planning Board Special Permit
columns.description: Permit number from Planning Board proceedings, if applicable. Not all projects require Planning Board approval.

columns.name: Building Permit
columns.description: Only projects with a status of Building Permit Granted or Complete list building permit numbers.  In cases of multiple building permits issued for a project either first or primary number listed.

columns.name: Total GFA
columns.description: Total gross floor area (GFA) for all uses included in project under development as defined by the zoning ordinance. Other gross floor area may exist on the parcel either in existing or prospective buildings that affects the floor-to-area ratio (FAR).

columns.name: Lot Area
columns.description: Includes land area of all parcels or property included in project.

columns.name: FAR
columns.description: Floor-to-Area (FAR) ratio, which is ratio of Total Gross Floor Area (GFA) to Lot Area. Other GFA not included in the project may exist on the parcel either in existing or prospective buildings that affects the FAR. See Note field for additional information about FAR in some cases.

columns.name: Primary Use
columns.description: Largest proportion of GFA by land use is primary use.  For more detail see the associated Project Use Data table.

columns.name: Developer
columns.description: Initially the applicant, the developer may change if ownership of the property changes.

columns.name: Residential Units
columns.description: Number of permitted residential units included in project including new construction and those in rehabbed structures. Blank value indicates nonresidential project.

columns.name: Affordable Units
columns.description: Number of residential units with affordability restrictions. Includes units qualified through all affordability programs. TBD indicates that the project will include a yet to be determined number of affordable units. None indicates that no affordable units are included in project.

columns.name: Hotel Rooms
columns.description: Blank value indicates project without hotel use.

columns.name: Neighborhood
columns.description: Cambridge is divided into thirteen neighborhoods. For more information see http://www.cambridgema.gov/CDD/planud/neighplan.aspx

description: The Development Log provides a record of large-scale development projects occurring in the City of Cambridge. description: This dataset contains the Average Daily Traffic counts and AM and PM peak hour Traffic counts measured on various road segments in the City of Cambridge from 1972 to 2017. The counts in this data set reflect only vehicle counts. An Average Daily Count measures the number of vehicles for an entire day, or an average of several days. Automatic Traffic Recorders (ATRs) log every vehicle that passes in each direction and this data is used to calculate Average Daily Traffic volumes and AM and PM Peak

name: Envision Cambridge Mobile Engagement Feedback

columns.name: Location of Engagement
columns.description: Location of the Mobile Engagement Station where this point was recorded. Note that the location of engagement tends to differ from the location of the topic discussed during the engagement. For example, a mobile engagement station at Fresh Pond in North Cambridge may have captured feedback about a location in Central Square. All coordinates listed in this dataset pertain to the topic of discussion rather than the location of mobile engagement stations.

columns.name: Latitude of Topic

A station located at Fresh Pond might have collected feedback about Central Square; this column would list the latitude of Central Square.

columns.name: Tag
columns.description: Qualitative coding of the comments by Envision Cambridge staff

A station located at Fresh Pond might have collected feedback about Central Square; this column would list the longitude of Central Square.

columns.name: Location of Topic
columns.description: Location of the topic being discussed. Note that this differs from the location of the mobile engagement station. For example, a mobile engagement station at Fresh Pond might collect feedback about a location in Central Square.

The Street Team asked participants at various locations across the City and throughout 2016 to mark a

description: This data set lists non-profit organizations with addresses in Cambridge, MA. Organizations qualify as non-profits if they have IRS recognition of tax-exempt status. Certain types of public charities, such as churches and organizations with less than $5,000 in gross receipts, are not required to file for official 501(c)(3) status and may not be listed here.

description: The data incorporates information on building permits, certificates of occupancy, lodging houses, and university owned housing, as well as Community Development Department information on the development pipeline. To assure equivalent treatment across the city, all apartment-style graduate student housing operated by local universities is included in the housing and property counts. These figures used here are a snap shot of the housing stock as of June 30, 2025.

name: EPA Air Quality Data - Pollutants

columns.name: Measurement Unit
columns.description: The unit of measure for the parameter.

columns.name: Event Type
columns.description: Indicates whether data measured during exceptional events (such as a wildfire) are included in the summary.

columns.name: Parameter Code
columns.description: The AQS (Air Quality System) code corresponding to the parameter (pollutant) measured by the monitor.

columns.name: Parameter
columns.description: The name or description assigned in AQS to the parameter measured by the monitor.

columns.name: Date
columns.description: The calendar date for the summary.

columns.name: Site Name
columns.description: The name of the site (if any) given by the State, local, or tribal air pollution control agency that operates it.

columns.name: Sample Duration
columns.description: The length of time that air passes through the monitoring device before it is measured.

columns.name: Pollutant Standard
columns.description: A description of the ambient air quality standard rules used to aggregate statistics.

columns.name: Method
columns.description: A short description of the processes, equipment, and protocols used in gathering and measuring the sample.

columns.name: Observation Count
columns.description: The number of observations (samples) taken during the day.

columns.name: Observation Percent
columns.description: The percent representing the number of observations taken with respect to the number scheduled to be taken during the day.

columns.name: Average
columns.description: The average (arithmetic mean) value for the day.

columns.name: Daily Maximum
columns.description: The highest value for the day.

columns.name: Hour of Maximum
columns.description: The hour (on a 24-hour clock) when the highest value for the day was taken.

columns.name: AQI
columns.description: The Air Quality Index for the day for the pollutant, if applicable.

description: Daily air quality data collected by the EPA Air Quality Service (AQS), from 1990-2021. This dataset includes air quality statistics from AQS monitors in the area surrounding Cambridge (Kenmore, Roxbury, Von Hillern, Chelsea). Each contains a parameter code which specifies one of the six pollutants for which the EPA AQS has an Air Quality Index (AQI). Information on how to interpret AQI values can be found here: https://www.airnow.gov/aqi/aqi-basics/

description: This dataset contains all of the ideas (projects) proposed by community members about how they think the City should spend a given year’s Participatory Budgeting funds. Each summer, community members submit ideas. Each fall, volunteer Budget Delegates research the ideas, evaluating each one based on feasibility, need, and impact. The Delegates ultimately develop about 20 final projects for the public to vote on in December of each year. To learn more, please visit http://pb.cambridgema.gov/

name: Medical Events

columns.name: Category
columns.description: MEDICAL CONDITIONS: Determines the severity of a call, and to bring a patient safely to the hospital. They include Chief Complaints like Headache, Abdominal Pain, Back Pain, and several others. It is the largest category. TRAUMA: Usually reported by an outside observer, rather than the person to whom the incident occurred. Typical Chief Complaints include Animal Bites, Falls, Lacerations, Motor Vehicle Accidents, and several others. TIME-LIFE SITUATION: High complexity, high priority calls. They include Chief Complaints like Breathing Problems, Choking, Lighting, Fainting, and several others.

columns.name: Coordinates
columns.description: The latitude and longitude of a point located within the footprint of the certified building(s).

columns.name: Passive House Final Certification Date
columns.description: The date of the Passive House final certification.

columns.name: Project Name
columns.description: Name of project with certified buildings.

columns.name: Match Address
columns.description: Street address(es) of certified building(s). If a single building has multiple street addresses, just one street address is reflected in this column. Some multi-building projects have just a single street address in this column but have multiple Building IDs listed in the Building ID(s) column.

columns.name: LEED Certified
columns.description: A ‘1’ in this column indicates that the project received a LEED certification that requires the whole-building to meet sustainability standards.

columns.name: LEED System and Version
columns.description: The LEED rating system and version used to obtain the LEED certification.

columns.name: LEED Certification Level
columns.description: The LEED certification level that a building achieved, based on the total number of points achieved. Possible certification levels are Certified, Silver, Gold and Platinum.

columns.name: LEED Certification Date
columns.description: The date that the building received the LEED certification.

columns.name: Passive House Certification Status
columns.description: The Passive House certification status. Only ‘Final Certified’ projects were included in this dataset.

columns.name: Passive House ID
columns.description: Passive House project number used to identify a Passive House project.

columns.name: Enterprise Green Communities Criteria Version
columns.description: The set of Enterprise Green Communities criteria by which the building was certified. Possible certification templates include Certification Workbook 2011, Certification Workbook 2015, and Certification Workbook 2020.

columns.name: Enterprise Green Communities Certification Date
columns.description: The date that the building received approval for the Enterprise Green Communities certification.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Certification Years
columns.description: The years in which a property attained ENERGY STAR certification.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Certified Scores
columns.description: The scores which enabled the project to attain ENERGY STAR certification. Eligible properties receive an ENERGY STAR score between 1-100, which compares the property to similar properties nationwide. A score of 50 represents median energy performance, while a score of 75 or higher is required to pursue ENERGY STAR certification.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Certification Category
columns.description: Property type (e.g. office, K-12 school, hotel, distribution center) which determines which buildings the property will be benchmarked against when determining ENERGY STAR scores.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Property ID
columns.description: A unique identifier for a building in ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager.

columns.name: Building ID
columns.description: The Cambridge GIS Building ID(s) that were covered by the sustainable building certifications.

columns.name: Notes
columns.description: Notes about the sustainable building certifications.

columns.name: Maplot
columns.description: Parcel ID (map and lot number) for the land parcel(s) that the certified building(s) are situated on.

columns.name: Other Vacant Units
columns.description: Number of vacant housing units that are not available through the rental or sale market. Units categorized as “other vacant” include those unoccupied units categorized by the Census Bureau as “Rented, Not Occupied”, “Sold, Not Occupied”, “For Seasonal, Recreational, or Occasional Use”, "For Migrant Workers” and “Other Vacant”. These units are unavailable to new permanent occupants through the for-rent or for-sale housing markets.

columns.name: Housing Units
columns.description: Total number of housing units in the aggregated Census block groups. A housing unit consists of separate living quarters where the occupants live apart from any other people in the building and that has direct access from the outside of the building or through a common hall. A housing unit may consist of a house, an apartment, a group of rooms, or a single room that is occupied (or if vacant, is intended for occupancy) as separate living quarters.

Vacant units include those set aside for part time occupancy, such as vacation and other second homes, temporary worker housing, homes for rent or for sale, homes rented or sold and awaiting occupancy, and vacant units that are not on the market.

columns.name: Median Family Income
columns.description: Median family income. The median divides the family income distribution into two equal parts: one-half of the cases falling below the median family income and one-half above the median. The median family income is based on the distribution of the total number of families including those with no income. A family consists of two or more people (one of whom is the householder) related by birth, marriage, or adoption residing in the same housing unit.

columns.name: Median Household Income
columns.description: Median household income. The median divides the household income distribution into two equal parts: one-half of the cases falling below the median household income and one-half above the median. The median household income is based on the distribution of the total number of households including those with no income. A household consists of all people who occupy a housing unit regardless of relationship. A household may consist of a person living alone or multiple unrelated individuals or families living together.

description: This dataset provides median income information for Cambridge neighborhoods, based on 2020-2024 American Community Survey estimates. columns.name: Population In Group Quarters %
columns.description: Percentage of the population residing in group quarters. Calculated by subtracting the population in households from the total population. Group quarters are places where people live or stay, in a group living arrangement that is owned or managed by an entity or organization providing housing and/or services for the residents. College/university dormitories are considered group quarters as are correctional facilities, juvenile facilities, nursing facilities, other health care facilities, military group quarters and other non-institutional facilities (homeless shelters, rehab facilities).

description: Participatory Budgeting (PB) is a democratic process through which community members directly decide how to spend part of a public budget. The City’s goals in implementing PB are to directly involve residents in the budgeting and city-building process, foster civic engagement and community spirit, and help ensure that the City’s capital plan reflects the priorities of residents. To learn more about PB in Cambridge or to get involved, please visit pb.cambridgema.gov.

description: The Traffic, Parking &Transportation department has conducted over 600 speed studies since 1987. Some locations have been studied more than once. The speed of each passing vehicle is recorded by radar for an hour each in the morning rush hour, evening rush hour, and midday. From these results, we find the 85th percentile speed for each one-hour period. The 85th percentile speed describes a speed that 85% of drivers are traveling slower than (and 15% of people are driving faster than). Traffic en

columns.name: Note
columns.description: Explanatory notes about parking arrangements, computation of FAR, project phasing, and other pertinent topics.

columns.name: Building Permit
columns.description: Only projects with a status of Building Permit Granted or Complete list building permit numbers.  In cases of multiple building permits issued for a project either first or primary number listed.

columns.name: Total GFA
columns.description: Total gross floor area (GFA) for all uses included in project under development as defined by the zoning ordinance. Other gross floor area may exist on the parcel either in existing or prospective buildings that affects the floor-to-area ratio (FAR).

columns.name: Lot Area
columns.description: Includes land area of all parcels or property included in project.

columns.name: FAR
columns.description: Floor-to-Area (FAR) ratio, which is ratio of Total Gross Floor Area (GFA) to Lot Area. Other GFA not included in the project may exist on the parcel either in existing or prospective buildings that affects the FAR. See Note field for additional information about FAR in some cases.

columns.name: Residential Units
columns.description: Number of permitted residential units included in project including new construction and those in rehabbed structures. Blank value indicates nonresidential project.

columns.name: Affordable Units
columns.description: Number of residential units with affordability restrictions. Includes units qualified through all affordability programs. TBD indicates that the project will include a yet to be determined number of affordable units. None indicates that no affordable units are included in project.

columns.name: Parking Spaces
columns.description: Includes only on site spaces assigned to uses found in the project. In some case parking is shared with other developments or is located off site. In some instances parking for other nearby uses is located on site. In these cases information about total parking found on the project parcel is stated in the Notes field.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: Primary street address of project. The street address of a project may change once a land subdivision takes place or an official address is assigned by the City of Cambridge.

columns.description: Refers to a name generally used to refer to the project.  Not all projects have names.

columns.name: Prior Site Name

columns.name: Neighborhood

columns.name: Developer
columns.description: Initially the applicant, the developer may change if ownership of the property changes.

columns.name: Project Stage
columns.description: Refers to the project stage.  Options are Permitting, Permit Granted or As of Right, Building Permit Granted, and Complete. See metadata for further details.

columns.name: Year Complete
columns.description: Refers to the year in which a Certificate of Occupancy is granted.

columns.name: Project Type
columns.description: Refers to the type of construction called for by the project. Options include addition, alteration, change of use, master plan, and new construction.

columns.name: Zoning
columns.description: Primary zoning category under which the project receives(d) permits.

columns.name: Hotel Rooms
columns.description: Blank value indicates project without hotel use.

columns.name: Planning Board Special Permit
columns.description: Permit number from Planning Board proceedings, if applicable. Not all projects require Planning Board approval.

columns.name: LatLong
columns.description: Latitude and longitude used to map project location. Determined using ArcGIS.

columns.name: Permit Type

description: Foreclosure is the process through which lenders recover the amount owed on a defaulted mortgage by selling or taking ownership of the property securing the loan. This data set tracks housing foreclosures at three points during the process: filing of a petition to foreclose, scheduling of a foreclosure auction, and filing of a foreclosure deed.

columns.description: Percentage of the population 25 years or older whose highest level of education is either an associates degree or who attended college but have not graduated

columns.name: Per Capita Income
columns.description: The average income received over the past 12 months for every resident in a geographic area, regardless of age or housing status. Calculated by dividing total income by total population.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: Latitude of the centerpoint of the neighborhood/city, based on official neighborhood/city boundaries.

columns.name: 2 Household Vehicles Available %
columns.description: Percentage of all households with 2 vehicles available for use by all members of a household

columns.name: 1 Household Vehicle Available %
columns.description: Percentage of all households with 1 vehicle available for use by all members of a household

columns.name: No Vehicles Available %
columns.description: Percentage of all households with no vehicles available for use by all members of a household

columns.name: Resident Commute: Work from Home %
columns.description: Percentage of workers 16 years or older (Cambridge residents only) who worked at home

columns.name: Resident Commute: Other %
columns.description: Percentage of workers 16 years or older (Cambridge residents only) who used a means not included in any other category above to commute to work, regardless of the location of place of work.

columns.name: Resident Commute: Motorcycle %
columns.description: Percentage of workers 16 years or older (Cambridge residents only) who rode a motorcycle to work, regardless of the location of place of work

columns.name: Resident Commute: Taxi or Ride-Hailing %
columns.description: Percentage of workers 16 years or older (Cambridge residents only) who took a taxi or ride-hailing service to work, regardless of the location of place of work

description: This dataset provides socio-economic information for Cambridge neighborhoods, based on 2020-2024 American Community Survey estimates. Because the U.S. Census Bureau does not publish data at the geographic level of Cambridge neighborhoods, 2020 Census block groups were aggregated to approximate neighborhood boundaries. In most cases, block group boundaries align closely with neighborhood boundaries; however, there are notable discrepancies - especially in neighborhoods 10 (West Cambridge), 12 (Ca

name: Street Trees

columns.name: Tree Grate
columns.description: This field is used to record if the metal tree grate surrounding the tree needs to be reset or removed. When the tree grows, it can get girdled by the tree grate.

columns.name: Street Name
columns.description: Name of the street for the address closest to the site.

columns.name: Overhead Wires
columns.description: Designates the presence of above-ground utility wires in the area of tree canopy growth at the site.

columns.name: ADA Compliant
columns.description: Designates whether the site is ADA compliant, for instance whether the site has the minimum required sidewalk width of 4 feet.

columns.name: Plant Date
columns.description: Date that a tree was last planted at this site.

columns.name: Street Number
columns.description: Number of the street address closest to the site.

columns.name: Ownership
columns.description: Designates the ownership of the tax parcel on which the site falls.

columns.name: Exposed Roots
columns.description: Indicates if the root flare of the tree is visible.

columns.name: TreeWellCo
columns.description: The type of cover, if any that is over the tree well. The two values are Tree Grate or Flexi-Pave.

columns.name: Memorial Tree
columns.description: Designates whether the current tree was planted in memoriam.

columns.name: Census Blocks 2010
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Census Blocks 2010' (e4yd-rwk4) the point in column 'the_geom' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: cambridge_cdd_zoning
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'cambridge_cdd_zoning' (swkg-bavi) the point in column 'the_geom' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: cambridge_neighborhoods
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'cambridge_neighborhoods' (rffn-qbt6) the point in column 'the_geom' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: Police Response Districts
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Police Response Districts' (v7jj-366k) the point in column 'the_geom' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: Police Neighborhood Regions
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Police Neighborhood Regions' (guic-hr4a) the point in column 'the_geom' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: Solar Rating
columns.description: Solar radiation rating between 1 and 5, where 1 is the lowest score and 5 is the highest (receives the most light). Pictometry 2014 DSM and DEM were used to determine tree height and measure shadows from 2014 3D buildings. SolarRating is the average of the solar radiation for both the DSM and DEM results for June, July, August, and September.

columns.name: Bare Root
columns.description: Designates whether the last tree was planted as a bare-root tree.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Designates whether the site is located along a street (Street Tree), beyond the back edge of a sidewalk in a front yard or other area not immediately adjacent to a street (Back of Sidewalk), in a park or other open space (Park Tree, Cemetery Tree, Golf Course), or on the grounds of a public school or other municipal building (Public School, City Building).

columns.name: Removal Date
columns.description: Date that a tree was last removed from this site.

columns.name: Site Type
columns.description: Designates whether the site is currently occupied by a tree (Tree) or stump (Stump), is an empty planting site (Planting Site), or has been paved over or is otherwise empty and no longer available for planting (Retired). Note: this field typically supersedes any conflicting data within the record; for example, a record designated as a “Planting Site” would indicate that any values for Diameter and Trunks were no longer valid.

columns.name: Trunks
columns.description: Number of tree trunks (see note at Diameter about using the number of trunks to calculate an average tree diameter).

columns.name: Geometry
columns.description: Geospatial layer geometry. The latitude and longitude data for the underlying map shapes.

columns.name: Abuts Open Space
columns.description: Indicates if the tree abuts an open space or if the tree well is the only open area around the tree.

columns.name: Diameter
columns.description: Tree diameter in inches taken at approximately 4.5 feet above ground (DBH). If the diameter point falls on a swelling in the trunk it is customary to measure below the swelling at the point where the diameter is smallest. Note: an average diameter should be calculated for trees with multiple trunks (see the Trunks field); an online calculator for multi-stemmed trees may be found at https://www.treetec.net.au/TPZ_SRZ_DBH_calculator.php.

columns.name: Cultivar
columns.description: Cultivated variety of the current tree species (see Species).

columns.name: Species Short
columns.description: Genus of the current tree species (see Species).

columns.name: Species
columns.description: Common name of the species of the current tree.

columns.name: Structural Soil
columns.description: Indicates if the tree was planted in structural soil.

name: Citywide Bicycle Counts

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: The latitude of the count location

columns.name: Cyclist Location
columns.description: The location of the cyclist may be: Street (travelling in the street or bike lane), Sidewalk (riding on the sidewalk), or Bikepath

columns.name: Year
columns.description: The year the count occurred

columns.name: Date
columns.description: The date the count occurred

columns.name: Time Period
columns.description: Identifies morning (AM) and evening (PM) count periods

columns.name: Time
columns.description: The start of the 15-minute interval represented in the count record

columns.name: Temperature
columns.description: If the time period is AM, the temperature in Fahrenheit degrees at 8:00 AM on the count date. If the time period is PM, the temperature at 5:00 pm on the count date.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: The longitude of the count location

columns.name: Count Location
columns.description: The assigned name for the count location. Where possible, this is the name used in the Cambridge GIS Intersections layer

columns.name: Street
columns.description: The street where the count occurred

columns.name: Cyclist Direction
columns.description: The direction of the cyclist in relation to the legal traffic direction. May be With Traffic, Against Traffic, or Bikepath

columns.name: Movement Type
columns.description: The specific movement made by the cyclists in this count, for example Left, Right, or Thru. Values vary by count location. "Red Light Running" indicates cyclists that ran the red light. Counts with these values are independent of the turnning movement - a cyclist running a red light to turn left would also be recorded under a "Left" turning movement for this location and time.

columns.name: Count
columns.description: The count of cyclists for this 15-minute period reflecting the associated location, year, time, street, traffic direction, cyclist location, cyclist direction, and movement type.

columns.name: Weather
columns.description: If the time period is AM, a description of the weather at 8:00 AM on the count date. If the time period is PM, a description of the weather at  5:00 pm on the count date.

columns.name: Notes
columns.description: Notes related to this count record. May note that the count occurred outside of the typical September count period, or that there was construction in the area during the count.

columns.name: Node Number
columns.description: The NodeNumber corresponding to the count location from the City GIS Intersections layer, if available.

columns.name: Traffic Direction
columns.description: The legal direction of traffic on the street where the count occurred

description: This bicycle count data is collected as part of the City of Cambridge’s regular citywide bicycle counts. These counts are typically done on weekdays (Tuesday-Thursday) in September, annually between 2002-2006 and every other year since 2008, with an additional count in 2019 due to poor weather in 2018. See also this web app with data visualization tools and more background information on using and interpreting the data.

columns.description: Total number of students enrolled in Cambridge Public Schools as of October 1 of the  school year. Only includes in-district students.

columns.name: Pct Economically-Disadvantaged (2015-2021)
columns.description: Percentage of students enrolled as of October 1 of the school year who are identified as  participating in one or more of the following state public assistance programs: (i) Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP); (ii) Transition Assistance for Families with Dependent Children (TAFDC); (iii) Department of Children and Families’ (DCF) foster care program; or (iv) MassHealth (Medicaid).

columns.name: School Year
columns.description: School year.

columns.name: Pct Low-Income (2022 to present)
columns.description: Percentage of students enrolled as of October 1 of the school year who meet any one of  the following criteria: (i) Identified as participating in one or more of the following state public assistance programs: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP); Transition Assistance for Families with Dependent Children (TAFDC); Department of Children and Families’ (DCF) foster care program; or Expanded MassHealth (Medicaid) up to 185% of the federal poverty level; (ii) Reported by the district as homeless; or (iii) Certified by the district as low-income through a supplemental data collection process.

name: Cambridge Municipal Greenhouse Gas Inventory 2008 - 2012 (Historical)

columns.name: UniqueID
columns.description: Row ID provided for ease of analysis. A concatenation of scope, year, fuel, and source.

columns.name: CO2e from CH4 (metric tons)
columns.description: Calculated value based on CH4 emissions in metric tons and global warming potential (GWP) factor for CH4. The Global Warming Potential (GWP) was developed to allow comparisons of the global warming impacts of different gases. Specifically, it is a measure of how much energy the emissions of one ton of a gas will absorb over a given period of time, relative to the emissions of one ton of carbon dioxide.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: The year during which emissions took place.

columns.name: Sector
columns.description: The process that generated greenhouse gas emissions.

columns.name: Use (kwh)
columns.description: Kilowatt hours. Energy use data in native units based on monthly electricity bills

columns.name: Use (therms)
columns.description: A unit of heat equivalent to 100,000 British Thermal Units (Btu) or 1.055 × 108 joules. Energy use data in native units based on monthly natural gas bills.

columns.name: Scope
columns.description: Scope 1, or direct emissions  are GHG emissions from fuels combusted within the city boundary. Scope 2, or indirect emissions, are GHG emissions occurring outside the City boundary, but as a consequence of the purchase and use of grid-supplied electricity, heat, steam and/or cooling within the city boundary

columns.name: Total CO2e (metric tons)
columns.description: Metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e). Calculated value based on energy use data tracked by the City in MassEnergyInsight, and emission factors and Global Warming Potential (GWP) factors approved by the The Climate Registry. The GWP was developed to allow comparisons of the global warming impacts of different gases. Specifically, it is a measure of how much energy the emissions of one ton of a gas will absorb over a given period of time, relative to the emissions of one ton of carbon dioxide.

columns.name: Fuel
columns.description: The fuel that generated greenhouse gas emissions.

columns.name: Use (MMBtu)

columns.name: CO2e from N2O  (metric tons)
columns.description: Calculated value based on NO2 emissions in metric tons and Global Warming Potential (GWP) factor for NO2 . GWP was developed to allow comparisons of the global warming impacts of different gases. Specifically, it is a measure of how much energy the emissions of one ton of a gas will absorb over a given period of time, relative to the emissions of one ton of carbon dioxide.

columns.name: Units
columns.description: Units measuring electricity use or electricity demand. Electricity use is measured in kilowatt hours (kWh). Electricity demand is measured in kilowatts (kW). Additionally, this dataset contains measurements for Power Factor and Reactive Power. Power Factor is the ratio of the real power that is flowing to the load and the apparent power that is supplied to the circuit. Reactive Power occurs in alternating current circuits when there is a phase difference between voltage and current. It is measured in kVAR for demand and kVARh for use.

description: The dataset contains vote results for all City Council agenda items in which a roll call vote was taken, starting in January of 2018. The Cambridge City Council uses two different voting methods: voice votes and roll call votes. During a voice vote, all those in favor of a motion verbally vote “yes” simultaneously, and then all those opposed vote “no” simultaneously. During a roll call vote, each Councilor votes “yes” or “no” in turn. Voice votes are more common and are not recorded in this data

name: Cambridge Community Wide Greenhouse Gas Inventory 2012

columns.name: Scope
columns.description: Greenhouse gas accounting protocols tend to assign a "scope" of 1, 2, or 3 to greenhouse gas emissions. The scope indicates whether the emissions derive from a source that is directly or indirectly controlled by Cambridge. Scope 1: All direct GHG emissions. Scope 2: Indirect GHG emissions from consumption of purchased electricity, heat or steam. Scope 3: Other indirect emissions, such as the extraction and production of purchased materials and fuels, transport-related activities in vehicles not owned or controlled by the reporting entity, electricity-related activities (e.g. T&D losses) not covered in Scope 2, outsourced activities, waste disposal, etc.

columns.name: Source
columns.description: The fuel or industrial process that leads to greenhouse gas emissions.

columns.name: Subsector
columns.description: Narrower, more specific economic activities that lead to greenhouse gas emissions.

columns.name: Carbon Dioxide Equivalent Emissions (MT CO2e)
columns.description: Each greenhouse gas contributes differently to global warming. In order to to compare different sources and sectors, greenhouse gas accounting protocols tend to convert greenhouse gases into carbon dioxide equivalent emissions. For example, the most recent Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change found one metric ton of methane creates 28 times more atmospheric warming than an equal amount of carbon dioxide over a 100 year period. This is known as methane's "global warming potential" (GWP). To convert from methane to CO2e, we multiple a given mass of methane by its GWP of 28. Nitrous oxide has a GWP of 265.

columns.name: Carbon Dioxide Emissions (MT CO2)
columns.description: Emissions of the greenhouse gas carbon dioxide (CO2). Units are megatonnes, or one million metric tons.

columns.name: Nitrous Oxide Emissions (MT N2O)
columns.description: Emissions of the greenhouse gas nitrous oxide (N2O). Units are megatonnes, or one million metric tons.

columns.name: Sector
columns.description: The broad economic activities that lead to greenhouse gas emissions.

columns.name: Methane Emissions (MT CH4)
columns.description: Emissions of the greenhouse gas methane (CH4). Units are megatonnes, or one million metric tons.

description: This dataset contains an inventory of Cambridge's estimated community-wide greenhouse gas emissions for the year 2012. The data was created by DNV-GL, MAPC and the Environmental & Transportation division of the Community Development Department. It was created to establish a baseline for total emissions community wide per the City’s commitment to the Global Covenant of Mayors. It provides a baseline for the City to set reduction goals and track progress towards those goals over time.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Category refers to the genre or artistic medium of the proposed project.  Cambridge Arts Grant categories are visual arts, film, video, dance, music, theater, literature, and multidisciplinary.

columns.name: Grant Type
columns.description: The type of grant.  There are two types of grants, fieldtrip grants and project grants.  Fieldtrip grants are used to purchase tickets for Cambridge youth to attend professional arts and cultural events in or outside the city of Cambridge.  Project grants support proposals from individuals and organizations engaged with the Cambridge community.  There are two subcategories for project grants, which are “Creating and Presenting” grants and “Education and Access” grants.  Creating and Presenting project grants support the development and or presentation of visual arts, dance, film/video, literature, music, theater, and new media arts.  Education and access grants promote in-depth understanding of and participation in the arts.  Education and access project grants also support projects that increase participation of populations that have limited access to the arts.

description: This data set contains moving van permits issued by the traffic & parking department. A moving van permit allows you to reserve curb space for the purpose of parking a vehicle on a City street while moving goods in or out of a building. Moving van permits can be issued for a maximum of two consecutive days. The permits are issued with time limits of 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. You can keep your van (or vehicle) in the space overnight if you’ve paid for two consecutive days.

description: The Water Department manages several "people counter" sensors at Fresh Pond Reservation (FPR). The sensors count visitors on the Perimeter Road, the main trail at FPR that circles Fresh Pond. Counters are also located at popular entrances to FPR. Some of the counters record both cyclists and pedestrians. However, most do not differentiate between user types. The sensors report user counts in 15-minute intervals. This dataset contains hourly aggregations of the 15-minute data. All counts include

name: City Council Election 2013

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Status at end of counting round.

columns.name: Total Votes
columns.description: Total votes for each candidate at the end of each counting round.

columns.name: Vote Distribution
columns.description: Number of votes each candidate received this counting round. After the first count, votes for elected and eliminated candidates  are redistributed to still-eligible candidates who have not reach the quota of 10 percent of votes plus 1. During the 2013 City Council Election, the quota was 1,775 votes.

columns.name: Candidate
columns.description: Candidate for 2013 City Council Election

columns.name: Count
columns.description: The vote count begins with the sorting of ballots by the first preference shown on each valid ballot. That is the NUMBER 1 vote on each ballot. This is generally known as the "First Count". Any candidates who reach the necessary quota with Number 1 votes are declared elected. Any extra ballots they receive beyond the quota are redistributed to the candidates marked next in preference (the number 2 preference) on those excess ballots. The count continues with the elimination of those candidates receiving fewer than fifty votes in the first count. Their ballots are redistributed to the other candidates according to the next preference marked. After each distribution, the candidate now having the lowest number of votes is eliminated and his/her ballots redistributed to the next indicated preference (number 2,3,4 etc.). Counting continues until nine candidates have been elected.

description: This dataset contains the original final vote tally from 2013 Cambridge City Council election. The City Council comprises nine members and is the Cambridge's lawmaking body. Proportional Representation (PR) is the method by which voters in Cambridge elect members of the City Council and School Committee. description: The dataset contains vote results for all City Council agenda items in which a roll call vote was taken, starting in January of 2016 and ending in December of 2017. The Cambridge City Council uses two different voting methods: voice votes and roll call votes. During a voice vote, all those in favor of a motion verbally vote “yes” simultaneously, and then all those opposed vote “no” simultaneously. During a roll call vote, each Councilor votes “yes” or “no” in turn. Voice votes are more common an

description: This data set contains traffic clearance permits. A Traffic Clearance Permit temporarily restricts parking for a specified length of time to provide the proper space needed for the turning radius of construction vehicles accessing a job site. This permit is also issued to provide space for re-routing traffic from a travel lane and into a parking lane associated with large construction jobs.

description: This dataset identifies locations of known solar installations. It includes both photovoltaic (PV) and solar hot water installations. The location may not fall in the exact placement of the solar equipment, especially on larger parcels, or parcels with equipment in multiple locations. This dataset only includes active installations. This dataset will be updated quarterly with new installations and to remove records for installations that have been retired.

description: This data set contains moving container or crate parking permits. A Moving Container/Crate Permit allows you to reserve curb space for the purpose of placing a crate or container on a City street while moving goods in or out of a building. These permits are issued for three consecutive days. Street Occupancy Permits are issued with time limits of 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. Your container or crate can be in place for the full three days.

Contractors may park vehicles with commercial license plates in Permit Parking Only locations for up to three (3) days without a permit when making a short-term repair at a resident’s home. description: This data set shows daily compost collection tonnage (collection is done on business days). Combined curbside residential, small business, and drop off food waste. The "route" is named for the day the compost is usually picked up (e.g. the "Monday route" is usually picked up on Mondays). The "date" column is the date the compost was actually picked up, whether or not that was on the usual weekday. description: This data set contains information about poems on sidewalks throughout Cambridge. Launched in 2015, Sidewalk Poetry invites Cambridge residents of all ages to submit their poetry for a chance to get their words stamped in concrete as part of the City’s sidewalk repair program. description: This data set contains information about the conservation and maintenance schedule of Cambridge's public art. The City of Cambridge has more than 280 works of contemporary public art, and Cambridge Arts takes care of them to preserve them for the future. Each spring our Conservation And Maintenance Program (CAMP) inspects, treats and cleans more than half of them.

name: PUBLICSAFETY_PoliceSectors
description: Police Sectors are areas which patrol cars are dispatched. In Cambridge there are 5 sector cars and each cruiser has two officers in each car. This is different from route cars which only have 1 officer per car. This layer is used for response by Cambridge Public Safety. Police Sectors are areas which police cars are dispatched using the computer aided dispatch (CAD) software.

name: TRAFFIC_CommercialParking
description: This layer contains point features of all Commercial parking lots in the City of Cambridge. These include private and municipal lots and garages and their addresses. Created for general use by City staff. Used for both mapping and specific identification of parking lot locations. For more information and download links see: http://www.cambridgema.gov/gis/gisdatadictionary/traffic/traffic_commercialparking.aspx

name: PUBLICSAFETY_FireEngineDistricts
description: Fire Engine Districts are areas which represent both Cambridge Engine companies housed in fire stations and areas which each engine will be dispatched. Some fire stations have Ladder companies and Rescue units which are represented in a different layer. Each Engine District is used by the Emergency Communication Center (ECC) to dispatch emergency vehicles and first responders.

name: PUBLICSAFETY_FireStations
description: This point layer contains the eight fire stations within the City of Cambridge. Created for internal use by the Cambridge Emergency Communications Department, for general map-making purposes, and for public distribution. For more information and download links see: http://www.cambridgema.gov/gis/gisdatadictionary/public_safety/publicsafety_firestations.aspx

name: ELECTIONS_StateRepDistricts
description: This polygon layer contains the six elective districts for state legislators who represent Cambridge in the Massachusetts House of Representatives. This layer was created for general use by the Cambridge Election Commission and for public distribution. Cambridge wards and precincts can be overlaid on district boundaries to designate which voters fall into each district. For more information and download links see: http://www.cambridgema.gov/gis/gisdatadictionary/elections/elections_staterepdistr

name: ELECTIONS_CongressionalDistricts
description: This polygon layer contains the two elective districts for congressmen in Cambridge in the United State Congress. This layer was created for general use by the Cambridge Election Commission and for public distribution. There are two congressional districts in Cambridge, one of which is in the middle of another one. For more information and download links see: http://www.cambridgema.gov/gis/gisdatadictionary/elections/elections_congressionaldistricts.aspx

name: PUBLICSAFETY_FireLadderDistricts
description: Fire Ladder Districts are areas which represent both Cambridge Ladder companies housed in fire stations and areas which each ladder truck will be dispatched. Each fire station has Engine companies. Some have Ladder companies. Each Ladder District is used by the Emergency Communication Center (ECC) to dispatch emergency vehicles and first responders.

name: PUBLICSAFETY_PoliceWalkingRoutes
description: Police Walking Routes are areas which officers patrol by foot. There are 37 different walking routes in Cambridge. This layer is used for response by Cambridge Public Safety.

name: LANDMARK_YouthCenters
description: This layer contains point features of Youth Centers in Cambridge. Created for general use by City staff. Used for both mapping and specific identification of buildings with a similar use. For more information and download links see: http://www.cambridgema.gov/GIS/gisdatadictionary/Landmark/LANDMARK_YouthCenters.aspx

name: PUBLICSAFETY_PoliceBusinessRegions
description: Police Business Regions are used for response and reporting by Cambridge Public Safety. These are used for reporting, mapping and analysis purposes. There are 8 regions in Cambridge.

name: PUBLICSAFETY_PoliceCarRoutes
description: Police Car Routes are areas which patrol cars are dispatched. In Cambridge there are many route cars and cruisers have one officer in each car. This is different from Sector cars which have 2 officers per car. This layer is used for response by Cambridge Public Safety.

name: PUBLICSAFETY_PoliceStation
description: This point layer contains the police stations within the City of Cambridge.

name: COVID-19 and Influenza Cases: Monthly Summary

columns.name: count
columns.description: The monthly case count. This field is null when suppressed for confidentiality (i.e., when the true count is between 1 and 4). True zeros are recorded as 0.

columns.name: disease
columns.description: Disease name (COVID-19 or Influenza).

columns.name: month
columns.description: Calendar month of the case count (1–12).

columns.name: suppressed
columns.description: Indicates whether the count is suppressed for confidentiality. true means the true count is between 1 and 4 and is not shown exactly.

columns.name: count_plot_value
columns.description: A plotting helper for charts. Equals the true count when not suppressed; equals 2.5 when suppressed (to allow suppressed values to appear on charts without revealing exact counts). Intended for visualization only.

columns.name: count_display
columns.description: Human-readable version of the monthly count for display purposes. Values include "0", "<5", or an exact number as text (e.g., "87").

columns.name: year
columns.description: Calendar year of the case count (e.g., 2024).

description: Monthly counts of COVID-19 and influenza cases among Cambridge residents, by disease and calendar month from 2018. Small counts are suppressed to protect confidentiality (displayed as "<5"), while true zeros are shown as 0. This dataset supports analysis of month-to-month trends and can be aggregated to calendar years or respiratory seasons.

description: This is a summarized version of the Cambridge Bluebikes Data (https://data.cambridgema.gov/Transportation/Cambridge-Bluebikes-Data/bdjh-g8un/about_data), which has records for every individual Bluebikes trip that started or ended in Cambridge. This version gives the counts of Bluebikes trips that started or ended at a Cambridge station by day. It is updated monthly along with the source data.

name: Communicable Disease Cases: Annual Summary

columns.name: year
columns.description: Calendar year of case count (e.g., 2024).

columns.name: count_display
columns.description: Human-readable version of the annual count for display purposes. Values include "0", "<5", or an exact number as text (e.g., "51").

columns.name: count
columns.description: The annual case count. This field is null when suppressed for confidentiality (i.e., when the true count is between 1 and 4). True zeros are recorded as 0.

columns.name: disease_category
columns.description: High-level grouping used for reporting and rollups (e.g., Respiratory, Enteric, Tickborne, Mosquitoborne, Bloodborne, Zoonosis, Other).

columns.name: disease
columns.description: Name of the reportable communicable disease/condition.

columns.name: suppressed
columns.description: Indicates whether the count is suppressed for confidentiality. true means the true count is between 1 and 4 and is not shown exactly.

columns.name: count_plot_value
columns.description: A plotting helper for charts. Equals the true count when not suppressed; equals 2.5 when suppressed (to allow suppressed values to appear on charts without revealing exact counts). This is intended for visualization only, not statistical analysis.

description: Annual counts of reportable communicable disease cases among Cambridge residents, by disease and calendar year. Small counts are suppressed to protect confidentiality (displayed as "<5"), while true zeros are shown as 0. This dataset supports the Cambridge Annual Communicable Disease Report and related public-facing summaries.
